title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
summary: Cursor announced Cursor Mobile, Origin (an agent-native Git platform), and updates to cloud agents at its inaugural Compile conference. The company also revealed a new model trained from scratch as part of its shift toward agent-first architecture.
writeup: This keynote from Cursor's inaugural Compile conference covers the company's product and architecture announcements. Michael Truell introduces Cursor Mobile, Origin (an agent-native Git platform), and updates to cloud agents, alongside a new model trained from scratch. The throughline is a shift toward agent-first architecture, with the foundation model signaling Cursor's move to build core technology in-house rather than rely solely on external models.
